name: The Light Went Out. So Did the Men Watching It.

video.title: The Light Went Out. So Did the Men Watching It.
video.global_style_suffix: dark carnival harlequin-noir illustration, bold flat vector shapes with thick confident ink outlines, harlequin diamond motifs and ornate vintage sideshow-poster borders, dramatic single-source spotlight cutting deep shadow, gaslight-gold and blood-crimson and bone-white on near-black aubergine, subtle velvet texture, macabre elegance, high contrast, clean uncluttered composition

direction_card.hook_style: open on the OBJECT (the exhibit) in unsettling detail before any people appear
direction_card.ending: return to the object, changed by what we now know

video.global_negative_prompt: photorealistic, 3d render, gore, blood splatter, realistic human faces, modern clothing, readable text, gibberish letters, cluttered, washed out, low contrast, neon, watermark

voice.speaker: Ryan
voice.instruct: Speak calmly and quietly, almost flat, like a tired keeper walking one guest through the exhibits after closing. Slow, even pace, small natural pauses, soft breaths between sentences; tiny hesitations and worn edges are welcome. Never theatrical, never excited. Let the facts do the frightening.

settings.music_vibe: calm dark ambient, faraway broken music box, low drones, slow airy pads, unhurried

scenes.narration: Three men watched this light. On December fifteenth, 1900, it went dark.
scenes.image_prompt: A lone lighthouse on a jagged black sea cliff at night, its lamp room dark and dead, cold moonlit waves far below, deserted, nobody around

scenes.narration: No distress call. No wreck. No bodies.
scenes.image_prompt: A vast empty night ocean under low storm clouds, one small dark rock island on the horizon, deserted — clearly showing distress call wreck bodies

scenes.narration: Just a shut gate, a cold stove, and a clock that had stopped.
scenes.image_prompt: A tall wall clock in a dim room, its pendulum hanging dead still, face turned into shadow, empty room

scenes.narration: Eilean Mor. Seventeen miles off Scotland. A rock the gulls own.
scenes.image_prompt: Aerial view of a tiny treeless island of black rock in a vast iron-grey sea, white gulls wheeling over it, deserted

scenes.narration: The lighthouse was one year old. Its light had never once failed.
scenes.image_prompt: A proud white lighthouse tower against a cold starry sky, warm golden beam sweeping out over dark water, nobody around

scenes.narration: Three keepers lived inside. One rule ruled them all.
scenes.image_prompt: A warm gaslit lighthouse kitchen window glowing small in the huge dark cliff face, three silhouettes at a table inside

scenes.narration: Someone always stays inside. Always.
scenes.image_prompt: A heavy lighthouse door standing ajar with warm light spilling onto wet stone steps, empty doorway, nobody around — clearly showing Someone always stays inside Always

scenes.narration: So why was Donald MacArthur's coat still hanging on its hook?
scenes.image_prompt: A single heavy black oilskin coat hanging on an iron hook in a dim stone hallway, one shaft of gaslight across it, nobody around

scenes.narration: James Ducat, principal keeper. Twenty-two years of lights. Four children ashore.
scenes.image_prompt: James Ducat standing at the lamp-room railing at dusk holding his cap to his chest, calm and steady

scenes.narration: Thomas Marshall, second assistant. Young, strong, new to the rock.
scenes.image_prompt: Thomas Marshall coiling heavy rope on a stone landing, wind whipping his oilskin coat

scenes.narration: And MacArthur, the stand-in. He wasn't supposed to be there at all.
scenes.image_prompt: Donald MacArthur seated close to an iron stove in his shirtsleeves and knitted vest, staring into the coals

scenes.narration: December fifteenth, nine in the morning. The log reads: all well.
scenes.image_prompt: A brass inkwell and quill beside a closed leather logbook on a wooden desk, cold morning light through a small round window, empty room

scenes.narration: That night, a passing steamer looked for the light. Nothing.
scenes.image_prompt: A dark steamship pitching on heavy night seas, tiny beneath a black horizon where a lighthouse beam should be, no light anywhere

scenes.narration: The captain logged it. The warning took three days to reach anyone.
scenes.image_prompt: A cramped ship's bridge lit by one swinging lantern, brass telegraph and empty wheel, nobody around — clearly showing captain logged warning took three days reach

scenes.narration: The relief boat was due on the twentieth. The storm said no.
scenes.image_prompt: Mountainous storm waves exploding over a small stone pier, white spray towering against black rocks, deserted

scenes.narration: Boxing Day, 1900. The Hesperus finally reached the island.
scenes.image_prompt: A small steam tender approaching the black island through choppy grey swell, coal smoke streaming flat from its funnel

scenes.narration: No flag on the pole. No boxes on the landing. No one waving.
scenes.image_prompt: An empty flagpole above a bare stone landing, steps rising into cold mist, deserted, nobody around

scenes.narration: They blew the whistle. Fired a rocket. The island didn't answer.
scenes.image_prompt: A white signal rocket bursting high over the dark island, its flare lighting empty cliffs, deserted

scenes.narration: Joseph Moore rowed ashore alone and climbed one hundred sixty steps.
scenes.image_prompt: Joseph Moore climbing a steep stone stairway zigzagging up a sheer cliff face, gripping the iron rail, seen from high above

scenes.narration: The gate was shut. The door was shut. His voice echoed back alone.
scenes.image_prompt: Joseph Moore pushing open a heavy iron compound gate, pure darkness waiting beyond it

scenes.narration: Inside: fire cold. Chairs tucked in place. The clock run down for days.
scenes.image_prompt: A tidy lighthouse kitchen gone grey and cold, three chairs neatly tucked at the table, thin dust hanging in one beam of light, empty room

scenes.narration: Two sets of oilskins were gone. The third still hung by the door.
scenes.image_prompt: Close-up of one lone oilskin coat on its hook beside two bare empty hooks, gaslight low, nobody around

scenes.narration: Upstairs, the lamp sat cleaned and filled. Ready to burn.
scenes.image_prompt: The great lighthouse lens crystal-clean in a cold lamp room, wick centered and unlit, frost creeping on the glass, empty

scenes.narration: Moore wrote that his knees shook. He begged not to be left there alone.
scenes.image_prompt: Joseph Moore standing pale in the dark kitchen doorway, cap crushed in both hands, eyes wide

scenes.narration: You've heard what the logbook supposedly said. Marshall crying. Ducat silent.
scenes.image_prompt: A quill lying across a shadowed desk, spilled ink pooled like a small black stain, one candle guttering, empty room — clearly showing You've heard logbook supposedly said Marshall crying

scenes.narration: The truth? Those entries were invented decades later, by a magazine.
scenes.image_prompt: A garish fan of 1920s pulp magazines spread on a table, lurid painted covers, deserted room

scenes.narration: The real evidence was waiting outside, at the west landing.
scenes.image_prompt: A narrow concrete landing carved into the cliff base, an iron crane above churning white foam, deserted

scenes.narration: Iron railings, bent flat like wire.
scenes.image_prompt: Extreme close-up of thick iron railings twisted and folded over on themselves, wet with sea spray

scenes.narration: A box of ropes, wedged one hundred ten feet up. Smashed open.
scenes.image_prompt: A shattered wooden crate high on a cliff shelf, heavy ropes spilled down the black rock face

scenes.narration: A stone heavier than a ton had simply moved.
scenes.image_prompt: A massive boulder sitting displaced on scarred rock, deep drag gouges in the stone behind it, deserted

scenes.narration: The sea had climbed higher that week than anyone believed water could.
scenes.image_prompt: A monstrous black wave towering the full height of the sea cliffs, moonlit spray tearing off its crest

scenes.narration: The verdict: a freak wave took Ducat and Marshall at the landing.
scenes.image_prompt: James Ducat and Thomas Marshall tiny on the stone landing beneath an immense rising wall of dark water

scenes.narration: And MacArthur, the man whose coat still hung, must have seen it coming.
scenes.image_prompt: Donald MacArthur's face in close-up at a rain-lashed window, eyes wide with horror, gaslight catching the wet glass

scenes.narration: He ran into a December gale in his shirtsleeves, shouting a warning.
scenes.image_prompt: Donald MacArthur sprinting down the cliff steps into the storm, soaked shirt clinging, mouth open mid-shout

scenes.narration: The wave was already there.
scenes.image_prompt: The cliff steps vanishing under an avalanche of white water, nobody visible — clearly showing wave already

scenes.narration: So says the report. But no one saw it. No bodies ever washed ashore.
scenes.image_prompt: The empty landing at grey dawn, dead calm sea, one loose rope trailing into the water, deserted

scenes.narration: The lamp stayed cleaned, filled, and ready. No one was left to light it.
scenes.image_prompt: The dark lamp room at night, the cold polished lens catching moonlight like a huge unblinking eye, empty

characters.name: James Ducat
characters.description: Principal lighthouse keeper, broad and steady, heavy navy-blue wool greatcoat with double rows of brass buttons, peaked keeper's cap, neat grey-streaked beard, weathered kind face

characters.name: Thomas Marshall
characters.description: Young second assistant keeper, lean and strong, glistening black oilskin coat and sou'wester hat, clean-shaven, wide alert eyes
characters.palette: black oilskin, storm grey, bone white

characters.name: Donald MacArthur
characters.description: Occasional stand-in keeper, thick-set and older, rolled white shirtsleeves and a dark knitted vest, no coat, heavy grey walrus moustache, deep-set haunted eyes

characters.name: Joseph Moore
characters.description: Slight young relief keeper, buttoned dark uniform coat, cap held in both hands, pale anxious face
characters.palette: dark navy, ash grey, pale white

titles: The Light Went Out. So Did the Men Watching It
The Light Went Out. So Did the Men Watching It: Every exhibit was real
seo.description:
Three men watched this light. On December fifteenth, 1900, it went dark.
Eilean Mor. Seventeen miles off Scotland. A rock the gulls own.
Boxing Day, 1900. The Hesperus finally reached the island.

The Midnight Menagerie — Every exhibit was real.
